unverified.label: Causality for Physical Interventions
unverified.quote: as agentic AI makes its way out of the realm of purely virtual and into the physical world where consequences could be much much more... important... you need to start thinking really hard about the difference between correlation and causation.

unverified.label: Industry Incentives Drive Usable Products
unverified.quote: I very quickly came to the realization that if you want to have a real impact, you need to build something that people would want to use... one had to be at a company because the incentives are just not aligned to do that within academia.

unverified.label: Measurement is the prerequisite to understanding biological systems
unverified.quote: you can't fix what you don't understand... and you can't understand what you don't measure

unverified.label: Interdisciplinary Dataset Design
unverified.quote: take those two groups put them in the room and say what are the really big questions that you wish you had a magic wand and would like to solve... let's think about how to design data sets that will allow the machine learning approaches to be trained effectively

unverified.label: The Three Buckets of Drug Discovery
unverified.quote: divide the process into three major buckets. One is what you think of as biology discovery... The middle third is, okay, well now we need to turn that hypothesis into an actual molecule... And then finally there's the enablement and acceleration of the clinical development process

unverified.label: The Data Printing Factory
unverified.quote: which is why we built the data printing factory that we have right now, which is how do we create at scale data... to drive our understanding of fundamental biology

unverified.label: Bits Meet Atoms
unverified.quote: Many people believe that A.I. is a magic wand that can allow you to move super fast. That assumption can be true in the virtual world... But it’s not true in the physical world, where bits meet atoms.

unverified.label: The Biologist vs. Engineer Mindset
unverified.quote: Biology still even today is a lot about the details. And the reason for this is that the exceptions... are often the starting point for new discovery. So, people kind of want to look for those, whereas engineers really care about let’s find the principles that cover 95% of what we see.

unverified.label: The Rowing Boat (Organizational Alignment)
unverified.quote: It's very different to lead a group of individuals each of whom has their own thing... versus building an organization and a company where people really have to row together in order for the boat to get to the right destination.

unverified.label: Data is not fungible
unverified.quote: Data is not fungible; web-scale data in one domain doesn't solve problems in another.

unverified.label: Measure to understand, understand to fix
unverified.quote: You can't fix what you don't understand... and you can't understand what you don't measure.

unverified.label: Team success vs. Individual success
unverified.quote: In a startup, you succeed or fail as a team; in academia, you succeed or fail as an individual.

all_results.label: True innovation happens at the boundaries of disciplines
all_results.quote: what you get if you put them in a room together and actually get to the point where they communicate with each other effectively, is that not only do you get better solutions, you get better problems.

all_results.label: Deliberate Cross-Disciplinary Architecture
all_results.quote: if you create a world in which those people are actually part of a team they develop a shared vocabulary and a shared Vision magic just happens

all_results.label: Drug failures primarily stem from pursuing the wrong biological target
all_results.quote: most of the things that fail in clinical development is because our therapeutic hypothesis was just wrong we don't understand the biology

all_results.label: Generate Fit-for-Purpose Data
all_results.quote: We need to generate the right data—data that is fit-for-purpose for machine learning.

all_results.label: Generate purpose-built data rather than just analyzing what exists
all_results.quote: instead of asking what can we do with the existing data it's asking what do we want to do and then let's generate the data so that we can do it well

all_results.label: Maximize your unique value and leverage
all_results.quote: i've always looked for opportunities where the experience the skills the the mindset that i bring have a disproportionate leverage i'm a big believer in leverage

all_results.label: Technology transfer requires moving people, not just publishing papers
all_results.quote: we realize that you can't really do Tech transfer with a paper you can do Tech transfer with a product

all_results.label: The privileged have a moral imperative to improve the world
all_results.quote: For those of us who had the privilege of being born to relatively affluent, well-educated families, where we didn’t need to struggle for where our next meal is coming from, that burden is actually even higher and we — we should be thinking about how we can give back.

all_results.label: AI Amplifies Rigorous Science
all_results.quote: Done right, A.I. is an amplifier of rigorous biology—not a substitute for it.

all_results.label: Anti-Hypothesis Driven Discovery
all_results.quote: We take a very different approach that is anti-hypothesis driven... rather than starting out with a hypothesis and then doing experiments to test those out we do experiments to feed into machine learning models who tell us what the interesting findings the interesting insights that emerge from those data

all_results.label: Causality for Physical Interventions
all_results.quote: as agentic AI makes its way out of the realm of purely virtual and into the physical world where consequences could be much much more... important... you need to start thinking really hard about the difference between correlation and causation.

all_results.label: Causality is necessary for drug development, but mechanism is not
all_results.quote: understanding cause and effect is absolutely necessary for drug development understanding the entirety of the why is... neither necessary or sufficient

all_results.quote: I very quickly came to the realization that if you want to have a real impact, you need to build something that people would want to use... one had to be at a company because the incentives are just not aligned to do that within academia.

all_results.label: Machine learning is the mathematical foundation for biology
all_results.quote: math is to physics as machine learning is to biology.

all_results.label: Measurement is the prerequisite to understanding biological systems
all_results.quote: you can't fix what you don't understand... and you can't understand what you don't measure

all_results.label: Pragmatism Over Elegance
all_results.quote: First of all, the focus of the work is not do you use the coolest, most elegant machine learning method, it's do you use the thing that works.

all_results.label: Stick to your principles over short-term gains
all_results.quote: i think sticking to your principles really allows you to when there is controversy to go back and say look our you know don't judge us by our words justice by our actions

The middle third is, okay, well now we need to turn that hypothesis into an actual molecule... And then finally there's the enablement and acceleration of the clinical development process

all_results.label: A.I.-First End-to-End Drug Discovery
all_results.quote: We have built an A.I.-first engine that transforms drug discovery and development end-to-end—from target discovery, through drug discovery, and into the clinic.

all_results.label: Bridging Data Modalities for Causal Predictions
all_results.quote: we've taken a rather unusual strategy of bringing together and bridging between two very distinct and complementary data modalities

all_results.label: Decision-Making for Maximum Impact
all_results.quote: if people are facing decision points i would encourage them to think about what do you bring to the table that is unique to the point that you can do something much better than the next best person

all_results.label: Intervening on Meeting Microaggressions
all_results.quote: it's okay for you to say yeah john that was a great idea and jane made that it's a very similar point a few moments ago and i think that is very very impactful

all_results.quote: For each class of models, the text describes the three fundamental cornerstones: representation, inference, and learning, presenting both basic concepts and advanced techniques.

all_results.quote: which is why we built the data printing factory that we have right now, which is how do we create at scale data... to drive our understanding of fundamental biology

all_results.label: The Exponential Curve of AI
all_results.quote: The thing about exponential curves is they are very misleading things. In the early stages people basically take the line between whatever we were last year, and this year and they interpolate linearly...

all_results.label: Causality vs. Correlation Lens

all_results.label: Drug Discovery as a Forked Road
all_results.quote: Each successful drug carries on its back the cost of all of those failures.

all_results.label: Illusion of Causality (Seductive Plausibility)
all_results.quote: My concern about A.I. in science isn’t the distant risk of superintelligence, but the erosion of rigor from the seductive plausibility of generative A.I.

So, people kind of want to look for those, whereas engineers really care about let’s find the principles that cover 95% of what we see.

all_results.quote: It's very different to lead a group of individuals each of whom has their own thing... versus building an organization and a company where people really have to row together in order for the boat to get to the right destination.

all_results.label: Avoid the golden hammer
all_results.quote: I think it's really important to avoid a mindset of I have an amazing hammer so everything must be a nail.

all_results.quote: rather than starting out with a hypothesis and then doing experiments to test those out we do experiments to feed into machine learning models

all_results.label: Judge by actions, not words

all_results.label: Measure to understand, understand to fix
all_results.quote: You can't fix what you don't understand... and you can't understand what you don't measure.

all_results.label: Sometimes XGBoost just works
all_results.quote: Sometimes it's just XGBoost just works and it's hard to beat.

all_results.label: Team success vs. Individual success
all_results.quote: In a startup, you succeed or fail as a team; in academia, you succeed or fail as an individual.

all_results.label: The 2-year vs 10-year technology estimation rule
all_results.quote: People tend to overestimate the value of any technology in the two-year time frame and underestimate it in a 10year time frame.

all_results.label: Use AI to win more, not fail faster
all_results.quote: While speed is important, failing faster is not helpful; our north star is using A.I. to win more.

all_results.label: AI as an amplifier
all_results.quote: Done right, A.I. is an amplifier of rigorous biology—not a substitute for it.

all_results.label: Anti-hypothesis driven approach
all_results.quote: We take a very different approach that is anti-hypothesis driven.

all_results.label: Better solutions and better problems
all_results.quote: What you get if you put them in a room together and actually get to the point where they communicate with each other effectively, is that not only do you get better solutions, you get better problems.

all_results.label: Bits meet atoms
all_results.quote: Many people believe that A.I. is a magic wand that can allow you to move super fast. That assumption can be true in the virtual world, where everything moves at the speed of computation. But it’s not true in the physical world, where bits meet atoms.

all_results.label: Building things people want
all_results.quote: I very quickly came to the realization that if you want to have a real impact, you need to build something that people would want to use.

all_results.label: Data fungibility
all_results.quote: Data is not fungible. The fact that we have web-scale data for text and images of cats and dogs and airplanes doesn't mean that we can just take that and transfer those capabilities like that to asking answering questions about human biology.

all_results.label: Disproportionate leverage
all_results.quote: i've always looked for opportunities where the experience the skills the the mindset that i bring have a disproportionate leverage i'm a big believer in leverage

all_results.label: Innovation at the boundary
all_results.quote: i've always believed that the most interesting innovation lies at the boundary of things because that's where you can really sort of bring in ideas from these different disciplines and create something that is truly transformative

all_results.label: Math is to physics as machine learning is to biology.
all_results.quote: Math is to physics as machine learning is to biology.

all_results.label: Regretting not trying
all_results.quote: Very few people regret having tried something and failed a lot of people regret not having tried.

all_results.label: Tech transfer requires a product
all_results.quote: You can't really do Tech transfer with a paper you can do Tech transfer with a product.

all_results.label: The burden of privilege
all_results.quote: For those of us who had the privilege of being born to relatively affluent, well-educated families, where we didn’t need to struggle for where our next meal is coming from, that burden is actually even higher and we — we should be thinking about how we can give back.

all_results.label: The danger of hyperbole
all_results.quote: I personally find hyperbole very problematic and I think hyperbole is something that is easy to fall prey to.

all_results.label: The erosion of scientific rigor
all_results.quote: My concern about A.I. in science isn’t the distant risk of superintelligence, but the erosion of rigor from the seductive plausibility of generative A.I.

all_results.label: The limits of the human brain in biology
all_results.quote: The human brain just cannot understand the human biology and its complexity.

all_results.label: The root cause of clinical failures
all_results.quote: Most of the things that fail in clinical development is because our therapeutic hypothesis was just wrong we don't understand the biology.
